name: Apple Books MCP
what_it_does: MCP server providing access to Apple Books library, highlights, and annotations. Enables AI agents to query your Apple Books reading library, access book metadata, retrieve highlights and notes made in books, and build AI-powered reading workflows — useful for knowledge management and second-brain systems.
use_cases: Querying book highlights and annotations from Apple Books for knowledge management agents
Building a reading knowledge base from Apple Books highlights for AI assistants
Retrieving book metadata and reading progress from personal library agents
Integrating Apple Books reading notes into note-taking and knowledge workflows
not_for: Non-Apple ecosystem users (Apple Books is macOS/iOS only)
Purchasing or downloading books via API
Teams requiring Windows or Android reading platform support
best_when: An Apple ecosystem user wants AI agents to access their Apple Books library and reading highlights — building a knowledge base from books or surfacing insights from reading notes.
avoid_when: You use Kindle, Kobo, or other e-readers. Apple Books is exclusively for Apple platforms. Also: read access only.

auth.notes: No authentication — reads from local Apple Books database on macOS. Requires macOS with Apple Books installed and synced.

known_agent_gotchas: macOS only — requires Apple Books database at standard macOS path
Requires macOS Full Disk Access or Books database access permission
Apple Books sync state must be current — highlights from iPhone/iPad may not sync immediately
Apple Books database schema may change with macOS updates — verify compatibility
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Apple Books database access. Requires macOS privacy permission for Books database. Personal reading data is private — no external transmission.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local database access. Personal reading data. macOS privacy permission required. No external access.
